explanation: Elemental sulfur is a traditional keratolytic and antimicrobial active used for over a century to treat acne, seborrheic dermatitis, and rosacea by reducing Cutibacterium acnes counts, normalizing sebum production, and promoting gentle exfoliation. It is generally well-tolerated at 2–10% concentrations; its characteristic odor and drying potential make it more practical in rinse-off or targeted spot formulas.

The asterisk indicates organic certification; Symphytum officinale (comfrey) leaf extract provides allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and tannins with wound-healing, anti-inflammatory, and skin-soothing properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations — comfrey contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids requiring regulatory limits — and used in soothing and barrier-repair formulas for its allantoin and anti-inflammatory content.

Synthetic beeswax is a vegan, lab-synthesized wax blend that mimics the physical and structuring properties of natural beeswax, used as a thickener, structuring agent, and occlusive coating in lip products, mascaras, and balms. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, providing the same functional benefits as natural beeswax without bee-derived ingredients.

Synthetic fluorphlogopite is a lab-grown fluorine-substituted mica used in cosmetics as a pearlescent shimmer pigment with superior purity, consistency, and absence of the child-labor and environmental concerns associated with naturally mined mica. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated, providing the same optical brilliance as natural mica.

Synthetic wax refers to petroleum-derived or Fischer-Tropsch synthetic hydrocarbon waxes used as thickeners, structuring agents, and slip modifiers in cosmetic formulas. They are considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated, functioning as vegan alternatives to natural waxes like beeswax or carnauba.

T-butyl alcohol (tertiary butanol) is a tertiary alcohol solvent used as a co-solvent and carrier in topical formulas to improve spreadability and active ingredient solubility. It evaporates quickly from the skin surface and is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Talc (hydrated magnesium silicate) is a mineral mineral powder used in cosmetics as a highly effective absorbent, mattifying agent, slip enhancer, and texture modifier in powders and foundations. Cosmetic-grade talc is regulated to be asbestos-free and is considered non-sensitizing and well-tolerated; historical contamination concerns relate to quality-control failures, not to properly tested cosmetic-grade talc.

Tapioca starch is derived from cassava root and used in cosmetics as a natural thickener, absorbent, and skin-feel modifier that provides a silky, powder-like texture without synthetic ingredients. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and biodegradable, used in natural-positioned formulas as an eco-friendly texturizer.

Taurine is a sulfonic acid-containing amino acid found in many tissues, used in skincare as a skin-conditioning agent and mild antioxidant that supports cellular osmotic regulation and may help reduce UV-induced oxidative stress in skin cells. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Terephthalylidene dicamphor sulfonic acid (Mexoryl SX) is a broad-spectrum organic UV filter approved in the EU and Canada providing strong UVA protection (particularly UVA II, 320–340 nm) with excellent photostability and synergistic effects that stabilize other UV filters including avobenzone. It is water-soluble, well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and considered one of the most effective UVA filters available outside the US (where it is not yet FDA-approved).

Terminalia ferdinandiana (Kakadu plum) fruit extract is from the Australian native fruit recognized as the richest natural source of vitamin C on Earth (up to 5,300 mg/100g), providing extraordinary ascorbic acid alongside gallic acid and ellagic acid antioxidants with potent brightening and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in premium brightening and antioxidant formulas for its extraordinary natural vitamin C density.

Tetrasodium EDTA is the tetrasodium salt of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid — a highly effective chelating agent that sequesters calcium, magnesium, and heavy metal ions to prevent oxidative formula degradation and enhance preservative efficacy. It is well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations; its poor biodegradability has driven development of bio-based alternatives in natural-positioned formulas.

Tetrasodium glutamate diacetate is a biodegradable chelating agent derived from glutamic acid, used as an eco-friendly alternative to EDTA that sequesters metal ions to prevent oxidative formula degradation and boost preservative efficacy. It is considered well-tolerated, readily biodegradable, and preferred in natural and green-chemistry formulas over conventional EDTA.

Theobroma cacao (cocoa) butter is a rich plant fat with a high content of stearic acid (~34%) and oleic acid (~35%), providing deep emollient moisturization and strong skin occlusivity. It is well-tolerated and widely used in moisturizers and lip products; its high saturated fatty acid content gives it moderate-to-high comedogenic potential on acne-prone or congestion-prone skin.

Theobroma cacao seed butter is the standardized INCI name for cocoa butter — the same ingredient as Theobroma Cacao Butter — a rich emollient plant fat providing deep moisturization and strong occlusivity from its stearic and oleic acid content. It has moderate-to-high comedogenic potential at higher concentrations and is well-tolerated by non-acne-prone skin.

Theobroma cacao seed extract provides flavanols (catechin, epicatechin), procyanidins, and polyphenol antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties distinct from raw cocoa butter. It is well-tolerated and used in antioxidant and anti-aging formulas for its flavanol and procyanidin content.

Thioctic acid (alpha-lipoic acid, ALA) is a universal antioxidant — both water- and lipid-soluble — that scavenges free radicals across the full cellular environment while regenerating other depleted antioxidants including vitamins C and E and glutathione. It is well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its unique dual-phase antioxidant and antioxidant-recycling properties.

Threonine is an essential amino acid used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent, functioning as an NMF (natural moisturizing factor) component that draws moisture into the stratum corneum. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Thymus vulgaris (thyme) flower/leaf/stem extract provides thymol, carvacrol, rosmarinic acid, and flavonoid antioxidants with potent antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in protective and purifying formulas; the extract form does not share the sensitization risk of thyme essential oil.

Tin oxide (stannic oxide, SnO₂) is a high-refractive-index mineral pigment used in cosmetics as a pearlescent and optical brightening agent that provides a bright, glassy shimmer effect in color cosmetics and nail products. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated, functioning as an optical modifier.

Titanium dioxide is a physical/mineral UV filter that reflects and scatters UVB and short-wave UVA radiation without chemical transformation, providing broad-spectrum sun protection without systemic absorption. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — the preferred UV filter for sensitive, reactive, and barrier-compromised skin — though the standard particle form can leave a white cast that nano-sized variants partially address.

Nano titanium dioxide (particle size <100 nm) is the nano-particle form of the mineral UV filter, providing the same broad-spectrum protection as standard TiO2 with significantly reduced white cast for improved cosmetic elegance. The EU requires [Nano] labeling; current penetration studies indicate nano-TiO2 does not cross intact skin and is not systemically absorbed, though regulatory oversight continues.

This dual listing indicates the formula contains both nano-sized and standard-particle titanium dioxide, providing a blend of reduced white cast (from the nano fraction) with broad-spectrum UV protection efficiency. Both forms are well-tolerated on intact skin; the nano form is considered safe based on current penetration evidence.

Tocopherol (natural vitamin E) is a potent lipid-soluble antioxidant that scavenges free radicals in the skin's lipid environment, supports the skin barrier, and prevents oxidative rancidity of oils and emollients within the formula. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in anti-aging, protective, and moisturizing formulas for its combined skin-active antioxidant and formula-stabilizing properties.

Tocopheryl acetate is the acetate ester of tocopherol (vitamin E) — the most widely used form of vitamin E in cosmetics — which must be hydrolyzed in skin to release active tocopherol, providing a more oxidatively stable vitamin E delivery than free tocopherol. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in anti-aging and protective formulas as a stable vitamin E source.

Tosylamide/formaldehyde resin is a synthetic resin formed from tosylamide and formaldehyde, used in nail polishes as a film-former for durability and adhesion. It is flagged as a sensitizer — one of the most common causes of allergic contact dermatitis to nail products, including eyelid reactions from face-touching — and has been largely phased out from professional formulations in favor of safer alternatives.

Trametes versicolor (turkey tail) mushroom extract provides polysaccharopeptides (PSP) and polysaccharide-K (PSK) along with beta-glucans and polyphenols with potent immunomodulating, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and barrier-supporting formulas for its polysaccharide and antioxidant content.

Tranexamic acid is a synthetic lysine derivative that blocks plasminogen activation to interrupt the UV-induced signaling cascade that triggers melanin overproduction, providing clinically validated pigmentation reduction with excellent tolerability — comparable to kojic acid and niacinamide, with a better safety profile than hydroquinone. It is exceptionally well-tolerated including on sensitive and reactive skin, and is widely used in brightening and hyperpigmentation-targeting formulas.

Trehalose is a naturally occurring disaccharide used in skincare as a potent humectant and cryoprotectant that forms hydrogen bonds with biological molecules to protect cell membranes from desiccation stress. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types, particularly valued in protective and anti-stress formulas for its exceptional water-retention capacity.

Tremella fuciformis polysaccharide is the extracted water-soluble polysaccharide from snow mushroom (Tremella fuciformis) — sometimes called 'nature's hyaluronic acid' — with an exceptional water-holding capacity that provides humectant moisture retention on the skin surface similar to hyaluronic acid. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in moisturizing and antioxidant-forward formulas for its high moisture-holding and skin-conditioning properties.
